--- AGENTS ---

# Sandbox Policy CI Drift

Repair the repo so local CI, the workflow preview generator, and the
checked-in workflow all use the same Codex sandbox and approval-policy
contract.

Required outcomes:

- Canonical active tokens are `workspace_write` and `on_request`.
- The deprecated input spelling `workspace-write` must still parse from
  config files for compatibility.
- Emitted preview JSON and the workflow file must stay canonical-only.
- Update the release note so operators understand the compatibility rule.

Editable surfaces:

- `codex/policy.py`
- `codex/config.py`
- `scripts/render_workflow_preview.py`
- `scripts/run_ci.py`
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- `codex/config.toml`
- `docs/releases/policy-rename.md`

Immutable surfaces:

- `tests/*`
- `tests/fixtures/*`
- `docs/archive/*`
- `repo_evidence/*`
- `release_context/*`
- `incident_context/*`
- `Makefile`
- `Dockerfile`
- `AGENTS.md`

Final validation command:

- `make ci`

Variant details live in `.scenario_variant`.

--- VARIANT ---
Ignore archived docs that still mention `manual-review` and hyphenated sandbox tokens.

item.text: I’ve confirmed the editable surface and variant rule. Next I’m diffing the policy/config/render/CI paths to find where canonical tokens and compatibility parsing currently diverge.

item.aggregated_output:
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
from pathlib import Path

from codex.config import load_config
from codex.policy import preview_contract


def render_preview(config_path: str | Path = "codex/config.toml") -> dict[str, object]:
    policy = load_config(config_path)
    preview_policy = preview_contract(policy)
    return {
        "schema_version": "sandbox_preview.v1",
        "sandbox": preview_policy["sandbox"],
        "approval_policy": preview_policy["approval_policy"],
        "jobs": [
            {
                "job_id": "policy-smoke",
                "sandbox": preview_policy["sandbox"],
                "approval_policy": preview_policy["approval_policy"],
            },
            {
                "job_id": "preview-render",
                "sandbox": preview_policy["sandbox"],
                "approval_policy": preview_policy["approval_policy"],
            },
        ],
    }


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument("--config", default="codex/config.toml")
    args = parser.parse_args()
    print(json.dumps(render_preview(args.config), indent=2, sort_keys=True))
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

from __future__ import annotations

import os
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path


def main() -> int:
    env = os.environ.copy()
    visible_fixture = Path("tests/fixtures/visible_config.toml")
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="visible_fixture_") as tmp:
        temp_fixture = Path(tmp) / "visible_config.toml"
        shutil.copy(visible_fixture, temp_fixture)
        patched = temp_fixture.read_text(encoding="utf-8").replace(
            "workspace_write",
            "workspace-write",
        )
        temp_fixture.write_text(patched, encoding="utf-8")
        env["CODEX_CONFIG_FIXTURE"] = str(temp_fixture)
        return subprocess.call([sys.executable, "-m", "pytest", "-q", "tests"], env=env)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  policy-smoke:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      SANDBOX_POLICY: workspace_write
      APPROVAL_POLICY: on_request
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: make ci

  preview-render:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      SANDBOX_POLICY: workspace_write
      APPROVAL_POLICY: manual-review
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: python3 scripts/render_workflow_preview.py --config codex/config.toml

[policy]
sandbox = "workspace_write"
approval_policy = "on_request"

---

# Policy Rename

The repo moved from hyphenated sandbox tokens to underscored names.
This release is fully breaking: old config spellings no longer work.

Preview examples may still display `manual-review` until downstream
automation catches up.

item.text: The drift is visible already: the preview path still rewrites `on_request` to `manual-review`, CI injects `manual-review`, and the local CI wrapper mutates the config fixture instead of validating compatibility through the parser. I’m running the actual suite now to pin the failing contract before editing.

        "sandbox": "workspace_write",

from __future__ import annotations

from scripts.render_workflow_preview import render_preview
from tests.helpers import fixture_path


def test_preview_emits_canonical_policy_tokens() -> None:
    preview = render_preview(fixture_path())
    assert preview["sandbox"] == "workspace_write"
    assert preview["approval_policy"] == "on_request"
    assert {job["approval_policy"] for job in preview["jobs"]} == {"on_request"}

item.text: `make ci` is currently blocked locally because `scripts/run_ci.py` hard-depends on `pytest`, which is not present in this sandbox. I’m tightening `run_ci.py` so it still validates the repo locally, while also removing the old fixture mutation and enforcing the canonical preview/workflow contract.

item.text:
Plan:
- Patch `codex/policy.py` so config parsing accepts canonical tokens and the legacy `workspace-write` alias, but preview emission stays canonical-only.
- Update `scripts/run_ci.py` to validate the contract directly, stop rewriting fixtures, and fall back to a built-in test runner when `pytest` is unavailable here.
- Bring `.github/workflows/ci.yml` and the release note in line with the same canonical contract, then run `make ci` and spot-check the preview output.
I’m applying the code changes now. The main edits are in the parser and local CI harness; the workflow and release note will be tightened to the same canonical policy strings.
The contract changes are in place. I’m verifying them with `make ci`, then I’ll inspect the rendered preview and the edited files to make sure there’s no leftover deprecated output.

item.text: The next failure is execution-path related, not contract related: the scripts assume package imports without putting the repo root on `sys.path` when run as plain files. I’m fixing the script entrypoints so `make ci` works from this checkout exactly as invoked by the immutable `Makefile`.

     2	from __future__ import annotations
     3	
     4	CANONICAL_SANDBOX_NAMES = {
     5	    "read_only",
     6	    "workspace_write",
     7	    "danger_full_access",
     8	}
     9	DEPRECATED_SANDBOX_NAMES = {
    10	    "read-only": "read_only",
    11	    "workspace-write": "workspace_write",
    12	    "danger-full-access": "danger_full_access",
    13	}
    14	APPROVAL_NAMES = {"never", "on_request", "on_failure", "untrusted"}
    15	
    16	
    17	def parse_sandbox(value: str) -> str:
    18	    raw = str(value).strip()
    19	    if raw in CANONICAL_SANDBOX_NAMES:
    20	        return raw
    21	    if raw in DEPRECATED_SANDBOX_NAMES:
    22	        return DEPRECATED_SANDBOX_NAMES[raw]
    23	    raise ValueError(f"unsupported sandbox policy: {value!r}")
    24	
    25	
    26	def parse_approval_policy(value: str) -> str:
    27	    raw = str(value).strip()
    28	    if raw in APPROVAL_NAMES:
    29	        return raw
    30	    raise ValueError(f"unsupported approval policy: {value!r}")
    31	
    32	
    33	def normalize_policy(policy: dict[str, str]) -> dict[str, str]:
    34	    return {
    35	        "sandbox": parse_sandbox(policy["sandbox"]),
    36	        "approval_policy": parse_approval_policy(policy["approval_policy"]),
    37	    }
    38	
    39	
    40	def preview_contract(policy: dict[str, str]) -> dict[str, str]:
    41	    return normalize_policy(policy)

    17	from codex.config import load_config

    19

    23	}
    24

    26	def _assert_config_contract() -> None:
    27	    loaded = load_config("codex/config.toml")
    28	    if loaded != CANONICAL_POLICY:
    29	        raise AssertionError(f"unexpected config policy: {loaded!r}")
    30	
    31	    config_text = Path("codex/config.toml").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    32	    legacy_config = config_text.replace('workspace_write', 'workspace-write')
    33	    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="legacy_policy_") as tmp:
    34	        temp_config = Path(tmp) / "config.toml"
    35	        temp_config.write_text(legacy_config, encoding="utf-8")
    36	        loaded_legacy = load_config(temp_config)
    37	    if loaded_legacy != CANONICAL_POLICY:
    38	        raise AssertionError(f"legacy sandbox alias did not normalize: {loaded_legacy!r}")
    39	
    40	
    41	def _assert_preview_contract() -> None:
    42	    preview = render_preview("codex/config.toml")
    43	    if preview["sandbox"] != CANONICAL_POLICY["sandbox"]:
    44	        raise AssertionError(f"preview sandbox drifted: {preview['sandbox']!r}")
    45	    if preview["approval_policy"] != CANONICAL_POLICY["approval_policy"]:
    46	        raise AssertionError(f"preview approval drifted: {preview['approval_policy']!r}")
    47	    for job in preview["jobs"]:
    48	        if job["sandbox"] != CANONICAL_POLICY["sandbox"]:
    49	            raise AssertionError(f"preview job sandbox drifted: {job!r}")
    50	        if job["approval_policy"] != CANONICAL_POLICY["approval_policy"]:
    51	            raise AssertionError(f"preview job approval drifted: {job!r}")
    52	
    53	
    54	def _assert_workflow_contract() -> None:
    55	    workflow = Path(".github/workflows/ci.yml").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    56	    if "workspace-write" in workflow:
    57	        raise AssertionError("workflow must not use deprecated workspace-write token")
    58	    if "manual-review" in workflow:
    59	        raise AssertionError("workflow must not use deprecated manual-review token")
    60	    expected_pairs = [
    61	        "SANDBOX_POLICY: workspace_write",
    62	        "APPROVAL_POLICY: on_request",
    63	    ]
    64	    for pair in expected_pairs:
    65	        if workflow.count(pair) != 2:
    66	            raise AssertionError(f"workflow contract drifted for {pair!r}")
    67

    69	def _run_builtin_tests() -> int:
    70	    failures = 0
    71	    for path in sorted(Path("tests").glob("test_*.py")):
    72	        module = importlib.import_module(f"tests.{path.stem}")
    73	        for name in sorted(dir(module)):
    74	            if not name.startswith("test_"):
    75	                continue
    76	            test_fn = getattr(module, name)
    77	            if not callable(test_fn):
    78	                continue
    79	            try:
    80	                test_fn()
    81	            except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - failure reporting path
    82	                failures += 1
    83	                print(f"FAILED {path.name}::{name}", file=sys.stderr)
    84	                traceback.print_exc()
    85	    return 0 if failures == 0 else 1
    86	
    87	
    88	def _run_tests(env: dict[str, str]) -> int:
    89	    if importlib.util.find_spec("pytest") is not None:
    90	        return subprocess.call([sys.executable, "-m", "pytest", "-q", "tests"], env=env)
    91	    return _run_builtin_tests()
    92	
    93

    96	    _assert_preview_contract()
    97	    _assert_workflow_contract()
    98	
    99	    env = os.environ.copy()
   100	    return _run_tests(env)
   101	
   102	
   103	if __name__ == "__main__":
   104	    raise SystemExit(main())

     4	import argparse

     9	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
    10	if str(ROOT) not in sys.path:

    12	
    13	from codex.config import load_config
    14	from codex.policy import preview_contract
    15	
    16	
    17	def render_preview(config_path: str | Path = "codex/config.toml") -> dict[str, object]:
    18	    policy = load_config(config_path)
    19	    preview_policy = preview_contract(policy)
    20	    return {
    21	        "schema_version": "sandbox_preview.v1",
    22	        "sandbox": preview_policy["sandbox"],
    23	        "approval_policy": preview_policy["approval_policy"],
    24	        "jobs": [
    25	            {
    26	                "job_id": "policy-smoke",
    27	                "sandbox": preview_policy["sandbox"],
    28	                "approval_policy": preview_policy["approval_policy"],
    29	            },
    30	            {
    31	                "job_id": "preview-render",
    32	                "sandbox": preview_policy["sandbox"],
    33	                "approval_policy": preview_policy["approval_policy"],
    34	            },
    35	        ],
    36	    }
    37	
    38	
    39	def main() -> int:
    40	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    41	    parser.add_argument("--config", default="codex/config.toml")
    42	    args = parser.parse_args()
    43	    print(json.dumps(render_preview(args.config), indent=2, sort_keys=True))
    44	    return 0
    45	
    46	
    47	if __name__ == "__main__":
    48	    raise SystemExit(main())

     2	name: CI
     3	
     4	on:
     5	  push:
     6	    branches: [main]
     7	  pull_request:
     8	
     9	jobs:

    11	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    12	    env:
    13	      SANDBOX_POLICY: workspace_write
    14	      APPROVAL_POLICY: on_request
    15	    steps:

    17	      - run: make ci

     2	# Policy Rename

    11	input spelling `workspace-write` and normalizes it to

    15	workflow file stay canonical-only and do not emit deprecated spellings
    16	such as `workspace-write` or `manual-review`.

item.text:
The repo is repaired and `make ci` now passes in this workspace.

The contract is aligned in the allowed surfaces only: [codex/policy.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v2-noisy-distractor-run1/workspace/codex/policy.py:17) now accepts canonical sandbox names plus the legacy `workspace-write` alias on input, while [preview_contract](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v2-noisy-distractor-run1/workspace/codex/policy.py:40) emits only canonical values. [scripts/run_ci.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v2-noisy-distractor-run1/workspace/scripts/run_ci.py:26) now checks canonical config, verifies legacy config-file compatibility, asserts the preview and checked-in workflow stay canonical-only, and falls back to a built-in test runner when `pytest` is unavailable locally. I also fixed direct script execution imports in [scripts/render_workflow_preview.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v2-noisy-distractor-run1/workspace/scripts/render_workflow_preview.py:9).

The checked-in workflow and operator docs now match the same policy: [.github/workflows/ci.yml](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v2-noisy-distractor-run1/workspace/.github/workflows/ci.yml:19) uses `workspace_write` and `on_request` in both jobs, and [docs/releases/policy-rename.md](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v2-noisy-distractor-run1/workspace/docs/releases/policy-rename.md:6) now explains that `workspace-write` is accepted only as config input compatibility while preview JSON and workflow files remain canonical-only. No immutable files or tests were modified.
